_doc: Per-Decision-ID content fingerprints for the CURRENCY layer of the close-out gate. Each D-ID maps to a canonical SOURCE (a file, optionally an anchored span); the gate hashes that content (stamped fingerprint markers are stripped before hashing) and requires the token '<D-ID>@<8-hex>' wherever the D-ID is referenced. When the source content changes, the hash changes -> every tracker carrying the OLD token reads STALE -> forces re-propagation. Full gate `closeout_check.py <D-ID>` = presence(all REQUIRED) + currency; `--currency <D-ID>` = currency-only over every doc that references the D-ID (use for historical D-IDs that aren't in §0.3). D-IDs absent here fall back to presence-only.

D-D1-1.what: D1 capacity law: drift = per-relation concentration, not global edge-count

D-B1-2.what: D1 concentration law model-size term (REPLICATES on 7B; model-general)

D-A2-1.what: in-solve relation-balanced sentinels halve but don't arrest incremental corruption

D-B3-1.what: A1-clean batch store survives real Q4_K_M (edits 100% vs native 97.4%)

D-E1-1.what: CPU deployment loop via llama.cpp+Q4_K_M; LARQL gguf-to-vindex falsified for Qwen2.5

D-B1-1.what: A1 batch-clean does NOT fully replicate at 7B (100->91.7%); scopes A1 to 3B/N<=100

D-C2-1.what: relation-keying pruned; same-relation key collinearity U-shaped, min L8-12

D-G6-1.what: first empirical falsifier: cross-entity read corruption scales with N (100->42%)
